audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Simeon, Zebulun, Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, Dan, and Joshua-inheritance allotment register, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Simeon's portion within Judah, Zebulun/Issachar/Asher/Naphtali/Dan city lists, Danite pressure and Leshem seizure, completion of tribal allotment, and Joshua's Timnath-serah inheritance.

verses.restored: Second lot came out for Simeon, for tribe of sons of Simeon according to their clans, and their inheritance was in midst of inheritance of sons of Judah.
For them in their inheritance were Beer-sheba, Sheba, Moladah,
Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem,
Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,
Ziklag, Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susah,
Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen: thirteen cities with their villages.
Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan: four cities with their villages,
and all villages around these cities as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of Negev. This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Simeon according to their clans.
From portion of sons of Judah was inheritance of sons of Simeon, for portion of sons of Judah was too much for them; so sons of Simeon inherited in midst of their inheritance.
Third lot came up for sons of Zebulun according to their clans; border of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.
Their border went up westward to Maralah, touched Dabbesheth, and touched wadi before Jokneam.
It turned from Sarid eastward, toward sunrise, upon border of Chisloth-tabor, went out to Daberath, and went up to Japhia.
From there it passed eastward, toward sunrise, to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and went out to Rimmon, marked toward Neah.
Border turned around it northward to Hannathon, and its end was Valley of Iphtah-el.
Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem: twelve cities with their villages.
This is inheritance of sons of Zebulun according to their clans, these cities with their villages.
Fourth lot came out for Issachar, for sons of Issachar according to their clans.
Their territory was Jezreel, Chesulloth, Shunem,
Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,
Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,
Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.
Border touched Tabor, Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and end of their border was Jordan: sixteen cities with their villages.
This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Issachar according to their clans, cities and their villages.
Fifth lot came out for tribe of sons of Asher according to their clans.
Their territory was Helkath, Hali, Beten, Achshaph,
Allammelech, Amad, and Mishal; it touched Carmel westward and Shihor-libnath.
It turned toward sunrise to Beth-dagon, touched Zebulun and Valley of Iphtah-el northward, Beth-emek and Neiel, and went out to Cabul on left,
and Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, and Kanah, as far as Great Sidon.
Border turned to Ramah and as far as fortified city of Tyre; border turned to Hosah, and its end was sea, by region of Achzib,
Ummah, Aphek, and Rehob: twenty-two cities with their villages.
This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Asher according to their clans, these cities with their villages.
Sixth lot came out for sons of Naphtali, for sons of Naphtali according to their clans.
Their border was from Heleph, from oak in Zaanannim, Adami-nekeb, Jabneel, as far as Lakkum, and its end was Jordan.
Border turned westward to Aznoth-tabor, went out from there to Hukkok, touched Zebulun on south, touched Asher on west, and Judah at Jordan toward sunrise.
Fortified cities were Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, Chinnereth,
Adamah, Ramah, Hazor,
Kedesh, Edrei, En-hazor,
Iron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh: nineteen cities with their villages.
This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Naphtali according to their clans, cities and their villages.
Seventh lot came out for tribe of sons of Dan according to their clans.
Border of their inheritance was Zorah, Eshtaol, Ir-shemesh,
Shaalabbin, Aijalon, Ithlah,
Elon, Timnah, Ekron,
Eltekeh, Gibbethon, Baalath,
Jehud, Bene-berak, Gath-rimmon,
Me-jarkon, Rakkon, with border opposite Joppa.

Border of sons of Dan went out beyond them; sons of Dan went up and fought with Leshem, captured it, struck it by edge of sword, possessed it, and dwelt in it. They called Leshem Dan, after name of Dan their father.
This is inheritance of tribe of sons of Dan according to their clans, these cities with their villages.
When they finished dividing land for inheritance by its borders, children of Israel gave inheritance in their midst to Joshua son of Nun.
According to mouth of YHWH they gave him city he asked, Timnath-serah in hill country of Ephraim. He built city and dwelt in it.

These are inheritances Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and heads of fathers' houses of tribes of children of Israel distributed by lot at Shiloh before YHWH, at entrance of tent of meeting. So they finished dividing land.

translation_notes: Simeon's inheritance is explicitly inside Judah's portion because Judah's share was too large, showing that allotments could be adjusted rather than fixed as simple absolute borders.
The chapter is dominated by administrative city lists whose totals and place identifications often require textual and historical-geography audit.
Coastal and northern cities such as Sidon, Tyre, Ekron, and Joppa show aspirational or complex territorial claims that should not be confused with secure control.
Dan's boundary 'going out beyond them' reflects pressure or insufficiency in its assigned territory and anticipates wider Danite migration traditions.
Leshem's capture, sword-striking, possession, and renaming as Dan is another conquest narrative and must not be romanticized as harmless relocation.
Joshua receives his inheritance only after tribal distributions are complete, presenting the leader as last served rather than first enriched.
Timnath-serah is granted by request and built by Joshua; leadership reward should not become dynastic entitlement or private extraction from the vulnerable.
The final line closes distribution at Shiloh before YHWH, but sacred process does not erase existing inhabitants or modern ethical limits.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 19 as a modern border charter, migration mandate, renaming permission, or conquest model. Center accountable distribution, the ability to adjust oversized shares, leader restraint, and the warning that administrative completion can still conceal violence and unresolved inhabitants.
